questions.question: Why does running a long-running task directly in a tkinter callback cause the GUI to freeze?
choices: tkinter automatically pauses rendering during callbacks
The long task blocks the mainloop, preventing it from processing events and redrawing widgets
Python's GIL prevents any GUI updates while Python code is executing
tkinter callbacks have a built-in 5-second timeout
questions.explanation: tkinter runs a single-threaded event loop (mainloop). When a callback performs a long computation, it monopolizes that thread, preventing mainloop from processing pending events, redraws, and user input until the callback returns.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to run a long task without freezing a tkinter GUI?
choices: Call root.update() in a tight loop inside the callback
Spawn the task in a separate thread and use the after() method to poll for results from the main thread
Use multiprocessing.Pool to parallelize the GUI event loop
questions.explanation: The standard pattern is to launch the long-running work in a background thread, then use widget.after(delay_ms, callback) to periodically check from the main thread whether the work is done. Only the main thread should update tkinter widgets, since tkinter is not thread-safe.

questions.question: Which method schedules a callback to run after a specified number of milliseconds, allowing periodic polling without blocking the event loop?
questions.explanation: widget.after(ms, callback) registers a one-shot timer that fires the callback after the given delay. Calling after() again inside the callback creates a repeating poll. after_idle() is similar but runs when the event loop is idle rather than after a fixed delay.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of wrapping reusable GUI components as custom widget classes that inherit from Frame?
choices: Frame subclasses run in their own thread automatically
They encapsulate layout and behavior into a self-contained, reusable component that can be embedded in any parent
Frame subclasses bypass the mainloop entirely
Only Frame subclasses can respond to keyboard events
questions.explanation: Subclassing Frame to create custom widget classes is a core tkinter design pattern. It bundles child widgets, layout logic, and event handlers into one reusable unit that can be attached to any parent container, promoting modular and maintainable GUI code.

questions.question: To copy text to the system clipboard in tkinter, you call widget.clipboard_clear() followed by widget.______(text).
questions.explanation: tkinter provides clipboard_clear() and clipboard_append(string) methods on any widget to interact with the system clipboard. This allows GUI applications to support copy/paste with other applications.

questions.question: When building a program that works as both a command-line tool and a GUI app, the common pattern is to separate the core ______ from the user interface so either front end can use it.
questions.explanation: Separating core application logic from the GUI (or CLI) layer is a fundamental design principle. The logic layer handles data processing and business rules, while the interface layer handles display and user interaction. This enables reuse and testability.
